commentaries.author: Cyprian of Carthage
commentaries.content: And the servant of the Lord ought not to strive; but to be gentle, docile to all men, patient with modesty, correcting those who resist, lest at any time God may give them repentance to the acknowledgment of the truth, and recover themselves from the snares of the devil, by whom they are held captive at his will."

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Vain speeches, or vain babbling. He seems to hint at disciples of Simon the magician, and their fables. (Witham)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: For they will not stop there. For when anything new has been introduced, it is ever producing innovations, and the error of him who has once left the safe harbor is infinite, and never stops.
